summary: "Add Contact" (the screen's primary CTA) is 100% client-side fake — it never calls POST /networking/contacts. The new contact appears to save (modal closes, card shows in the "New" column) but is pure ephemeral React state; a reload permanently loses it.
reproduction: Login as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/networking.
Click "+ Add Contact".
Fill Name="MV-networking-Contact-<ts>", Role, Company; click "Save Contact".
Observe: modal closes, contact card appears immediately in the pipeline's New column.
Open browser devtools network tab / capture: zero POST requests to /networking/contacts (or any endpoint) fired during the click.
Reload the page (F5).
Observe: the contact is gone. GET /networking/contacts confirms zero rows were ever created.
expected: Save Contact persists the contact via POST /networking/contacts (the documented, working backend endpoint); the contact survives reload and is visible via GET /networking/contacts.
observed: No network call fires on Save. Contact exists only in local component state (`added` array in page.tsx). Confirmed gone after reload in two independent fresh sessions.

summary: Outreach Queue and Communication Log cards do not render real backend data correctly: a field-name contract mismatch between the frontend TS interface / JSX (`to`/`subject`/`preview`/`tone` and `when`/`who`/`channel`/`note`) and the actual GET /workspaces/networking/summary response (`contactName`/`company`/`subject`/`kind`/`status`/`scheduledAt`/`sentAt`) leaves most fields blank. The Communication Log is effectively illegible even with real "sent" data present.
reproduction: Login, create a real contact via POST /networking/contacts (e.g. name=MV-networking-Test, company=MV Co).
Create a real outreach task via POST /networking/outreach with contact_id=<that contact>, type=follow_up (stays status=pending).
Navigate to /dashboard/networking.

Observe the Outreach Queue card: subject line ("Follow Up — MV Co") renders, but the recipient-name line above it is blank and the `tone:` badge shows "tone:" with no value; message preview line is blank.
PATCH the outreach task to status=sent; reload.
Observe the Communication Log card: entry renders as blank/garbled — no who, no channel, no note text, only separator punctuation (" · ", " — ") visible.
expected: Real outreach/communication data (contact name, message preview, channel/tone, timestamp) renders legibly, since the backend has and returns this data.
observed: Backend response field names never match what the frontend reads; 3 of 4 fields blank in Outreach Queue, 4 of 4 fields blank in Communication Log.

summary: "Import from LinkedIn" button (empty state) does not perform any LinkedIn import or OAuth — it opens the identical manual Add Contact modal as the header's "+ Add Contact" button. No LinkedIn integration is reachable anywhere on this screen. Also a wireframe deviation: wireframe shows two distinct empty-state buttons ("Import from LinkedIn" + "Add Contact Manually"); shipped build has only one, mislabeled button.
reproduction: Login, navigate to /dashboard/networking?demo=empty (or use an account with 0 contacts).
Click "Import from LinkedIn" (LinkedIn-branded button).
Observe: the generic "Add Contact" modal (Name/Role/Company fields) opens — same as manual add, no LinkedIn OAuth redirect, no connection list.
expected: Either a real LinkedIn OAuth/import flow, or (if not built) a button honestly labeled for manual add, or a disabled/coming-soon state — not a mislabeled affordance.
observed: Button click opens the manual single-contact form; label and icon falsely imply bulk LinkedIn import.

summary: "Review all drafts" button in the Outreach Queue card has no click handler at all (confirmed in source, apps/web/src/app/dashboard/networking/page.tsx) — clicking it is a complete dead-end: no navigation, no modal, no console/network event, no visible feedback.
reproduction: Login, seed a real outreach task via POST /networking/outreach so the Outreach Queue renders.
Navigate to /dashboard/networking.
Click "Review all drafts".
Observe: nothing happens — URL unchanged, no modal, no request fired.
expected: Navigates to a drafts review view/modal, or is visually disabled if the feature doesn't exist yet.
observed: Button is inert; live-verified with real outreach data present in two independent sessions.
evidence: test-artifacts/session-a-log.json (STEP4_REVIEW_DRAFTS_BUTTON — button absent in empty state, as expected)

summary: No contact-detail view exists anywhere in the reachable UI. Contact cards are not clickable/interactive. There is no UI control to edit an existing contact's stage, add notes, or set a follow-up reminder — the only ways to mutate a contact (PATCH) or reach a real create (POST, since the modal is fake per MV-networking-001) are direct API calls, unreachable from any button, link, or menu.
reproduction: Login, seed 1-2 real contacts via POST /networking/contacts.
Navigate to /dashboard/networking; observe cards render in the pipeline.
Click a contact card.
Observe: no navigation, no modal, no detail panel — nothing happens.
Search the whole page for any edit/stage-change/notes/reminder control: none exists.
expected: Per the wireframe's product intent ("Recruiter & Referral CRM") and the BRIEF's explicit test scope (contact detail, tags/status/relationship stage, notes, follow-up reminders), a user should be able to open a contact and manage these fields from the UI.
observed: Zero UI surface for contact detail/edit/notes/reminders; entirely read-only display + a non-persisting fake create.
evidence: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/networking/page.tsx (full file reviewed — no onClick/detail affordance on contact-card article)

summary: Pipeline cards omit the wireframe's stage-specific status indicators: wireframe shows a green "Call Thu" schedule badge on Scheduled-stage cards and a "Referred" checkmark badge on Placed-stage cards; shipped build renders only name/role-company/warmth-stars for every stage regardless of stage-specific state.
reproduction: Compare design/screens/networking.html contact-nw11 (Scheduled, shows "Call Thu" badge) and contact-nw12 (Placed, shows "Referred" badge) against the live pipeline for contacts in "meeting"/"referral" stage.
observed: No such badges exist in the shipped component for any stage.

summary: OutreachTask referential integrity is not enforced despite the DDL declaring `"contactId" text REFERENCES "Contact"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE`. (a) POST /networking/outreach with a nonexistent contact_id returns 201 (should reject). (b) Deleting a Contact does not cascade-delete its OutreachTask rows — orphans remain.
reproduction: POST /networking/outreach with contact_id="nonexistent-mv-test-id" -> observe HTTP 201 (not 404/422).
POST /networking/contacts to create contact C; POST /networking/outreach referencing C -> task T created.
DELETE /networking/contacts/{C.id} -> 204.
GET /networking/outreach -> T is still present, contactId still points at the deleted C.id.
expected: FK constraint rejects orphan creation, and/or cascade-deletes dependent tasks on contact deletion, matching the declared schema.
observed: Neither protection is active in production; orphan OutreachTask rows accumulate silently, which will worsen the illegible-rendering issue in MV-networking-002 over time.

summary: emailAgent is not wired or reachable anywhere on the Networking screen. No "Connect Gmail" affordance, no outreach-drafting AI trigger, no agent-generated draft content exists in the shipped UI (the wireframe's "Agent drafted a warm follow-up..." copy is mockup-only). Since no agent entry point exists, agent-integration testing (real generation vs. fixture, quota decrement, audit fields, honest progress/error states) could not be performed from this screen — there is no human gate to describe because the feature isn't reachable at all, gated or otherwise.
reproduction: Login, navigate to /dashboard/networking (both empty and populated states, incl. with real outreach data).
Inspect full page HTML/DOM and all network calls for any emailAgent trigger, Gmail-connect CTA, or draft-generation action.
None found.
expected: BRIEF flagged emailAgent as possibly wired here; if so, an honest connect/run/paywall-gated flow should be reachable.
observed: No such flow exists on this screen in the current build.
evidence: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/networking/page.tsx (full file reviewed — no agent-run or Gmail-connect code path)

summary: Clicking "Cancel" in the Add Contact modal closes the modal without adding a contact (correct), but does NOT reset the `form` component state. Reopening the modal shows the previously-typed, cancelled text still populating the Name field.
reproduction: Click "+ Add Contact"; type Name = "MV-networking-ShouldBeDiscarded".
Click "Cancel". Modal closes; contact correctly not added to pipeline.
Click "+ Add Contact" again.
Observe: Name field still shows "MV-networking-ShouldBeDiscarded" from the previous, cancelled attempt.
expected: Reopening after Cancel shows a fresh, empty form.
observed: Stale form state persists across Cancel/reopen. Reproduced identically in two independent fresh runs.

summary: Pressing Escape while the Add Contact modal is open does not close it, despite an `onKeyDown` handler in source specifically checking for `e.key === "Escape"`. (X button and Cancel button both work correctly as alternate close paths.)
reproduction: Click "+ Add Contact" to open the modal.
Press the Escape key.
Observe: modal remains open.
expected: Escape closes the modal (standard dialog behavior; also explicitly coded for in source).
observed: Modal does not close on Escape in production. Reproduced identically in two independent fresh runs. Likely cause: the dialog root never receives programmatic focus on open, so the keydown handler attached to that div never fires (Escape targets document body instead).
evidence: test-artifacts/scripts/nw-session-e-modal-controls.cjs (run twice, identical result both times: escapeCloses:false)
